gold.rationale: The article's central game result is invented, and the visuals are used to falsely authenticate it. One image is generated, another is edited to simulate a live broadcast frame, and the reused celebration photo is miscaptioned as evidence from the alleged 2026 UConn-Duke Elite Eight game.

Braylon Mullins delivered a game-winning 35-footer with 0.4 seconds remaining to overcome a 19-point deficit.

retrieved_evidence.text: WASHINGTON, D.C. — UConn is Final Four-bound after freshman Braylon Mullins buried a 35-footer with 0.4 seconds left to stun Duke 73-72 in the Elite Eight on March 29. AP reports the Huskies rallied from 19 down.

Team materials and roster information circulated during the NCAA tournament identify Braylon Mullins as a freshman guard for UConn during the 2025–26 season. According to staff familiar with the program, Mullins had been used primarily in a backcourt role, combining perimeter shooting with on-ball responsibilities in limited minutes earlier in the year. Coaches quoted after the March 29, 2026 Elite Eight game against Duke referred to his contributions as part of a broader group effort by UConn’s younger players in the national spotlight.

According to accounts from team staff and media present courtside, UConn guard Braylon Mullins is said to have converted a long-distance shot from approximately 35 feet with 0.4 seconds remaining in regulation of the Elite Eight contest on March 29, 2026. Early descriptions from broadcasters and arena statisticians indicated that the attempt came from well beyond the three-point line and followed a hurried inbounds sequence. Officials noted that the play was briefly reviewed to confirm timing and footing before the score was added to the official record.

Descriptions of the aftermath of UConn’s reported Elite Eight triumph over Duke emphasize moments of players and staff gathering on the court amid falling confetti, a common motif in NCAA Tournament celebrations when a team secures a place in the Final Four. In these celebratory accounts, athletes in UConn uniforms are portrayed embracing, shouting and raising their arms toward the stands, while arena personnel and fans react around them. Observers note that such jubilation has been associated with the moment Braylon Mullins’ 35-foot buzzer-beater was said to have clinched UConn’s 73–72 win and confirmed its spot in the 2026 national semifinals.

In recent reports about UConn’s 73–72 victory over Duke in the Elite Eight, particular attention has been given to the closing seconds as documented by television-style score graphics. Accounts describe a scoreboard display showing Duke 72 and UConn 73 with less than a second remaining, alongside a broadcast lower-third identifying the matchup and late-game situation. According to commentators, such details have been used to reconstruct how UConn preserved its narrow lead after Braylon Mullins’ long-range buzzer-beater, setting up the program’s return to the men’s Final Four in the 2026 NCAA Tournament.

The outcome of UConn’s 73-72 Elite Eight victory over Duke on March 29, 2026 is being framed by school officials and tournament commentators as sending the Huskies back to the Final Four. According to statements from the program, the win secured UConn another appearance in the national semifinals, extending a period of sustained success in the competition. Organizers noted that travel plans, ticket allocations and media availabilities for the Huskies’ next game at the Final Four site would be finalized in the days following the regional final.

UConn edged Duke 73-72 in an NCAA men’s basketball Elite Eight game played on March 29, 2026, according to game reports from tournament officials. The matchup, held at a neutral-site regional venue, was described by observers as tightly contested in the closing minutes, with multiple lead changes and extended video review sequences. Statistical summaries circulated by the organizing body indicated that UConn’s narrow victory secured the program a regional championship and continued its recent run of deep tournament appearances, while Duke’s season concluded with the single-possession loss.

UConn’s dramatic path toward the 2026 men’s Final Four has been described through accounts of a tight NCAA Tournament Elite Eight matchup in which freshman guard Braylon Mullins, wearing jersey number 24, took a deep attempt in the closing seconds against Duke’s blue-clad defense. In these descriptions, arena lighting, the illuminated scoreboard and animated reactions from teammates and coaches along the bench underscore the high-stakes atmosphere typical of late-game March Madness moments, as observers highlight how this long-range effort became central to narratives about UConn’s one-point win and advancement.

Tournament recaps released this week describe UConn as having rallied from a deficit of about 19 points in its Elite Eight matchup against Duke on March 29, 2026. According to game flow data compiled by NCAA statisticians, the team trailed by double digits for an extended stretch in the first half before gradually reducing the margin after halftime. Coaches and players later told reporters that defensive adjustments and improved rebounding were viewed internally as key factors in the late turnaround, though full analytical breakdowns are still being compiled.

In the aftermath of UConn’s one-point Elite Eight win over Duke on March 29, 2026, college basketball analysts quickly began referring to the matchup as one of the defining finishes of this year’s NCAA tournament. According to early television ratings and online viewership figures cited by broadcasters, the closing sequence drew significant attention on highlight programs and social media platforms. Commentators suggested that the dramatic final possession and narrow margin of victory would likely secure the game a lasting place in seasonal retrospectives.

text_analysis.alignment_reasoning: The post text claims the game occurred on March 29, 2026, but the image descriptions and OCR overlays explicitly show the date as March 29, 2023, creating a material temporal mismatch.
text_analysis.t3_evidence: The post text states the game was played on 'March 29, 2026', whereas the image descriptions for image_1 and image_2 contain OCR text '2023' and '0.4 FINAL SECONDS' confirming the event took place in 2023.
t3_problematic_subclaims: The post claims the date was March 29, 2026, but the visual evidence (image_1, image_2) displays '2023' on the scoreboard overlay.
text_analysis.c1_evidence: The post text describes image_1 as showing 'Mullins throws his arms up on the floor after the game-winner', but the image description states the scene is 'A wide shot of a basketball game in progress... A player... is mid-air shooting the ball'.
c1_problematic_subclaims: The post claims image_1 shows Mullins celebrating with arms up, but the image description shows a player mid-air shooting the ball.
text_analysis.global_assessment: The post is materially misaligned due to a fabricated future date (2026) conflicting with 2023 visual evidence and a semantic mismatch between the described celebration and the actual gameplay image.

judge.level3_rationale: 尽管文章描述了真实的比赛事件，但图片显示的是2023年的比赛而非2026年，且图片1展示的是投篮瞬间而非庆祝，图片3展示的是女子球员而非男子球员，这些关键细节的错置导致图文严重不符。
judge.reasoning_trace: The post claims a 2026 game, but images show 2023. Image 1 is mislabeled as celebration but shows a shot. Image 3 shows women, not men. This is a classic Mixture case of real event text paired with mismatched images.
judge.core_claim: UConn freshman guard Braylon Mullins hit a last-second shot to beat Duke 73-72 in the Elite Eight on March 29, 2026, sending them to the Final Four.
key_subclaims: The game took place on March 29, 2026.
The final score was 73-72 in favor of UConn.
The game was the Elite Eight round.
Image 1 shows Braylon Mullins celebrating with his arms up after the game-winner.
Image 2 shows Braylon Mullins (number 24) reacting after the game.
Image 3 shows UConn players celebrating on the court with confetti.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The game took place on March 29, 2026.
subclaim_findings.note: The scoreboard in Image 1 explicitly displays '2023 DI MEN'S BASKETBALL CHAMPIONSHIP' and the date '2023', contradicting the text's claim of 2026.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The final score was 73-72 in favor of UConn.
subclaim_findings.note: Image 1 shows a score of 74-73 with 1.0 seconds left, while Image 2 shows 72-73 with 0.4 seconds left. The text claims 73-72. The images show a different game state (tied or UConn leading by 1) than the text's specific final score claim, but the context of a close Elite Eight game is supported.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The game was the Elite Eight round.
subclaim_findings.note: The scoreboard in Image 1 clearly reads 'ELITE EIGHT'.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: Image 1 shows Braylon Mullins celebrating with his arms up after the game-winner.
subclaim_findings.note: The text claims the image shows Mullins throwing his arms up on the floor after the game-winner. The image description and visual evidence show a player in a navy blue jersey (number 24) jumping to shoot the ball, with defenders contesting the shot. This is the moment of the play, not the celebration.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: Image 2 shows Braylon Mullins (number 24) reacting after the game.
subclaim_findings.note: The image shows a player in a navy blue jersey with 'UCONN' and number 24. The overlay confirms the score 72-73 and '0.4 FINAL SECONDS', matching the context of the game described.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: Image 3 shows UConn players celebrating on the court with confetti.
subclaim_findings.note: The image shows female basketball players celebrating with confetti. The text describes UConn players celebrating. While the team and context (celebration) match, the gender of the players in the image contradicts the text's description of the men's basketball game.

judge.verdict_reasoning: The post contains a significant temporal mismatch (claiming 2026 vs images showing 2023) and misidentifies the action in Image 1 (celebration vs shooting). Furthermore, Image 3 depicts female players, contradicting the men's basketball context. These errors constitute a mix of misleading context and cross-modal inconsistencies that prevent a True or Mostly True rating.

image_findings.explanation: The image is from the 2023 championship (scoreboard says 2023), but the text claims the 2026 game. Additionally, the text claims the image shows celebration, but the image shows the player shooting the ball.

The image shows the correct player (Mullins #24) and context (Elite Eight, 0.4 seconds), but the scoreboard overlay indicates the year is 2023, creating a temporal mismatch with the text's 2026 claim.
The text describes UConn men's basketball players celebrating, but the image shows female basketball players celebrating.
